001// Copyright (c) FIRST and other WPILib contributors.
002// Open Source Software; you can modify and/or share it under the terms of
003// the WPILib BSD license file in the root directory of this project.
004
005package edu.wpi.first.math.kinematics;
006
007import static edu.wpi.first.units.Units.MetersPerSecond;
008import static edu.wpi.first.units.Units.RadiansPerSecond;
009import static edu.wpi.first.units.Units.Seconds;
010
011import edu.wpi.first.math.geometry.Pose2d;
012import edu.wpi.first.math.geometry.Rotation2d;
013import edu.wpi.first.math.geometry.Translation2d;
014import edu.wpi.first.math.geometry.Twist2d;
015import edu.wpi.first.math.kinematics.proto.ChassisSpeedsProto;
016import edu.wpi.first.math.kinematics.struct.ChassisSpeedsStruct;
017import edu.wpi.first.units.Angle;
018import edu.wpi.first.units.Distance;
019import edu.wpi.first.units.Measure;
020import edu.wpi.first.units.Time;
021import edu.wpi.first.units.Velocity;
022import edu.wpi.first.util.protobuf.ProtobufSerializable;
023import edu.wpi.first.util.struct.StructSerializable;
024import java.util.Objects;
025
026/**
027 * Represents the speed of a robot chassis. Although this class contains similar members compared to
028 * a Twist2d, they do NOT represent the same thing. Whereas a Twist2d represents a change in pose
029 * w.r.t to the robot frame of reference, a ChassisSpeeds object represents a robot's velocity.
030 *
031 * <p>A strictly non-holonomic drivetrain, such as a differential drive, should never have a dy
032 * component because it can never move sideways. Holonomic drivetrains such as swerve and mecanum
033 * will often have all three components.
034 */
035public class ChassisSpeeds implements ProtobufSerializable, StructSerializable {
036  /** Velocity along the x-axis. (Fwd is +) */
037  public double vxMetersPerSecond;
038
039  /** Velocity along the y-axis. (Left is +) */
040  public double vyMetersPerSecond;
041
042  /** Represents the angular velocity of the robot frame. (CCW is +) */
043  public double omegaRadiansPerSecond;
044
045  /** ChassisSpeeds protobuf for serialization. */
046  public static final ChassisSpeedsProto proto = new ChassisSpeedsProto();
047
048  /** ChassisSpeeds struct for serialization. */
049  public static final ChassisSpeedsStruct struct = new ChassisSpeedsStruct();
050
051  /** Constructs a ChassisSpeeds with zeros for dx, dy, and theta. */
052  public ChassisSpeeds() {}
053
054  /**
055   * Constructs a ChassisSpeeds object.
056   *
057   * @param vxMetersPerSecond Forward velocity.
058   * @param vyMetersPerSecond Sideways velocity.
059   * @param omegaRadiansPerSecond Angular velocity.
060   */
061  public ChassisSpeeds(
062      double vxMetersPerSecond, double vyMetersPerSecond, double omegaRadiansPerSecond) {
063    this.vxMetersPerSecond = vxMetersPerSecond;
064    this.vyMetersPerSecond = vyMetersPerSecond;
065    this.omegaRadiansPerSecond = omegaRadiansPerSecond;
066  }
067
068  /**
069   * Constructs a ChassisSpeeds object.
070   *
071   * @param vx Forward velocity.
072   * @param vy Sideways velocity.
073   * @param omega Angular velocity.
074   */
075  public ChassisSpeeds(
076      Measure<Velocity<Distance>> vx,
077      Measure<Velocity<Distance>> vy,
078      Measure<Velocity<Angle>> omega) {
079    this(vx.in(MetersPerSecond), vy.in(MetersPerSecond), omega.in(RadiansPerSecond));
080  }
081
082  /**
083   * Creates a Twist2d from ChassisSpeeds.
084   *
085   * @param dtSeconds The duration of the timestep.
086   * @return Twist2d.
087   */
088  public Twist2d toTwist2d(double dtSeconds) {
089    return new Twist2d(
090        vxMetersPerSecond * dtSeconds,
091        vyMetersPerSecond * dtSeconds,
092        omegaRadiansPerSecond * dtSeconds);
093  }
094
095  /**
096   * Discretizes a continuous-time chassis speed.
097   *
098   * <p>This function converts a continuous-time chassis speed into a discrete-time one such that
099   * when the discrete-time chassis speed is applied for one timestep, the robot moves as if the
100   * velocity components are independent (i.e., the robot moves v_x * dt along the x-axis, v_y * dt
101   * along the y-axis, and omega * dt around the z-axis).
102   *
103   * <p>This is useful for compensating for translational skew when translating and rotating a
104   * swerve drivetrain.
105   *
106   * @param vxMetersPerSecond Forward velocity.
107   * @param vyMetersPerSecond Sideways velocity.
108   * @param omegaRadiansPerSecond Angular velocity.
109   * @param dtSeconds The duration of the timestep the speeds should be applied for.
110   * @return Discretized ChassisSpeeds.
111   */
112  public static ChassisSpeeds discretize(
113      double vxMetersPerSecond,
114      double vyMetersPerSecond,
115      double omegaRadiansPerSecond,
116      double dtSeconds) {
117    // Construct the desired pose after a timestep, relative to the current pose. The desired pose
118    // has decoupled translation and rotation.
119    var desiredDeltaPose =
120        new Pose2d(
121            vxMetersPerSecond * dtSeconds,
122            vyMetersPerSecond * dtSeconds,
123            new Rotation2d(omegaRadiansPerSecond * dtSeconds));
124
125    // Find the chassis translation/rotation deltas in the robot frame that move the robot from its
126    // current pose to the desired pose
127    var twist = Pose2d.kZero.log(desiredDeltaPose);
128
129    // Turn the chassis translation/rotation deltas into average velocities
130    return new ChassisSpeeds(twist.dx / dtSeconds, twist.dy / dtSeconds, twist.dtheta / dtSeconds);
131  }
132
133  /**
134   * Discretizes a continuous-time chassis speed.
135   *
136   * <p>This function converts a continuous-time chassis speed into a discrete-time one such that
137   * when the discrete-time chassis speed is applied for one timestep, the robot moves as if the
138   * velocity components are independent (i.e., the robot moves v_x * dt along the x-axis, v_y * dt
139   * along the y-axis, and omega * dt around the z-axis).
140   *
141   * <p>This is useful for compensating for translational skew when translating and rotating a
142   * swerve drivetrain.
143   *
144   * @param vx Forward velocity.
145   * @param vy Sideways velocity.
146   * @param omega Angular velocity.
147   * @param dt The duration of the timestep the speeds should be applied for.
148   * @return Discretized ChassisSpeeds.
149   */
150  public static ChassisSpeeds discretize(
151      Measure<Velocity<Distance>> vx,
152      Measure<Velocity<Distance>> vy,
153      Measure<Velocity<Angle>> omega,
154      Measure<Time> dt) {
155    return discretize(
156        vx.in(MetersPerSecond), vy.in(MetersPerSecond), omega.in(RadiansPerSecond), dt.in(Seconds));
157  }
158
159  /**
160   * Discretizes a continuous-time chassis speed.
161   *
162   * <p>This function converts a continous-time chassis speed into a discrete-time one such that
163   * when the discrete-time chassis speed is applied for one timestep, the robot moves as if the
164   * velocity components are independent (i.e., the robot moves v_x * dt along the x-axis, v_y * dt
165   * along the y-axis, and omega * dt around the z-axis).
166   *
167   * <p>This is useful for compensating for translational skew when translating and rotating a
168   * swerve drivetrain.
169   *
170   * @param continuousSpeeds The continuous speeds.
171   * @param dtSeconds The duration of the timestep the speeds should be applied for.
172   * @return Discretized ChassisSpeeds.
173   */
174  public static ChassisSpeeds discretize(ChassisSpeeds continuousSpeeds, double dtSeconds) {
175    return discretize(
176        continuousSpeeds.vxMetersPerSecond,
177        continuousSpeeds.vyMetersPerSecond,
178        continuousSpeeds.omegaRadiansPerSecond,
179        dtSeconds);
180  }
181
182  /**
183   * Converts a user provided field-relative set of speeds into a robot-relative ChassisSpeeds
184   * object.
185   *
186   * @param vxMetersPerSecond The component of speed in the x direction relative to the field.
187   *     Positive x is away from your alliance wall.
188   * @param vyMetersPerSecond The component of speed in the y direction relative to the field.
189   *     Positive y is to your left when standing behind the alliance wall.
190   * @param omegaRadiansPerSecond The angular rate of the robot.
191   * @param robotAngle The angle of the robot as measured by a gyroscope. The robot's angle is
192   *     considered to be zero when it is facing directly away from your alliance station wall.
193   *     Remember that this should be CCW positive.
194   * @return ChassisSpeeds object representing the speeds in the robot's frame of reference.
195   */
196  public static ChassisSpeeds fromFieldRelativeSpeeds(
197      double vxMetersPerSecond,
198      double vyMetersPerSecond,
199      double omegaRadiansPerSecond,
200      Rotation2d robotAngle) {
201    // CW rotation into chassis frame
202    var rotated =
203        new Translation2d(vxMetersPerSecond, vyMetersPerSecond).rotateBy(robotAngle.unaryMinus());
204    return new ChassisSpeeds(rotated.getX(), rotated.getY(), omegaRadiansPerSecond);
205  }
206
207  /**
208   * Converts a user provided field-relative set of speeds into a robot-relative ChassisSpeeds
209   * object.
210   *
211   * @param vx The component of speed in the x direction relative to the field. Positive x is away
212   *     from your alliance wall.
213   * @param vy The component of speed in the y direction relative to the field. Positive y is to
214   *     your left when standing behind the alliance wall.
215   * @param omega The angular rate of the robot.
216   * @param robotAngle The angle of the robot as measured by a gyroscope. The robot's angle is
217   *     considered to be zero when it is facing directly away from your alliance station wall.
218   *     Remember that this should be CCW positive.
219   * @return ChassisSpeeds object representing the speeds in the robot's frame of reference.
220   */
221  public static ChassisSpeeds fromFieldRelativeSpeeds(
222      Measure<Velocity<Distance>> vx,
223      Measure<Velocity<Distance>> vy,
224      Measure<Velocity<Angle>> omega,
225      Rotation2d robotAngle) {
226    return fromFieldRelativeSpeeds(
227        vx.in(MetersPerSecond), vy.in(MetersPerSecond), omega.in(RadiansPerSecond), robotAngle);
228  }
229
230  /**
231   * Converts a user provided field-relative ChassisSpeeds object into a robot-relative
232   * ChassisSpeeds object.
233   *
234   * @param fieldRelativeSpeeds The ChassisSpeeds object representing the speeds in the field frame
235   *     of reference. Positive x is away from your alliance wall. Positive y is to your left when
236   *     standing behind the alliance wall.
237   * @param robotAngle The angle of the robot as measured by a gyroscope. The robot's angle is
238   *     considered to be zero when it is facing directly away from your alliance station wall.
239   *     Remember that this should be CCW positive.
240   * @return ChassisSpeeds object representing the speeds in the robot's frame of reference.
241   */
242  public static ChassisSpeeds fromFieldRelativeSpeeds(
243      ChassisSpeeds fieldRelativeSpeeds, Rotation2d robotAngle) {
244    return fromFieldRelativeSpeeds(
245        fieldRelativeSpeeds.vxMetersPerSecond,
246        fieldRelativeSpeeds.vyMetersPerSecond,
247        fieldRelativeSpeeds.omegaRadiansPerSecond,
248        robotAngle);
249  }
250
251  /**
252   * Converts a user provided robot-relative set of speeds into a field-relative ChassisSpeeds
253   * object.
254   *
255   * @param vxMetersPerSecond The component of speed in the x direction relative to the robot.
256   *     Positive x is towards the robot's front.
257   * @param vyMetersPerSecond The component of speed in the y direction relative to the robot.
258   *     Positive y is towards the robot's left.
259   * @param omegaRadiansPerSecond The angular rate of the robot.
260   * @param robotAngle The angle of the robot as measured by a gyroscope. The robot's angle is
261   *     considered to be zero when it is facing directly away from your alliance station wall.
262   *     Remember that this should be CCW positive.
263   * @return ChassisSpeeds object representing the speeds in the field's frame of reference.
264   */
265  public static ChassisSpeeds fromRobotRelativeSpeeds(
266      double vxMetersPerSecond,
267      double vyMetersPerSecond,
268      double omegaRadiansPerSecond,
269      Rotation2d robotAngle) {
270    // CCW rotation out of chassis frame
271    var rotated = new Translation2d(vxMetersPerSecond, vyMetersPerSecond).rotateBy(robotAngle);
272    return new ChassisSpeeds(rotated.getX(), rotated.getY(), omegaRadiansPerSecond);
273  }
274
275  /**
276   * Converts a user provided robot-relative set of speeds into a field-relative ChassisSpeeds
277   * object.
278   *
279   * @param vx The component of speed in the x direction relative to the robot. Positive x is
280   *     towards the robot's front.
281   * @param vy The component of speed in the y direction relative to the robot. Positive y is
282   *     towards the robot's left.
283   * @param omega The angular rate of the robot.
284   * @param robotAngle The angle of the robot as measured by a gyroscope. The robot's angle is
285   *     considered to be zero when it is facing directly away from your alliance station wall.
286   *     Remember that this should be CCW positive.
287   * @return ChassisSpeeds object representing the speeds in the field's frame of reference.
288   */
289  public static ChassisSpeeds fromRobotRelativeSpeeds(
290      Measure<Velocity<Distance>> vx,
291      Measure<Velocity<Distance>> vy,
292      Measure<Velocity<Angle>> omega,
293      Rotation2d robotAngle) {
294    return fromRobotRelativeSpeeds(
295        vx.in(MetersPerSecond), vy.in(MetersPerSecond), omega.in(RadiansPerSecond), robotAngle);
296  }
297
298  /**
299   * Converts a user provided robot-relative ChassisSpeeds object into a field-relative
300   * ChassisSpeeds object.
301   *
302   * @param robotRelativeSpeeds The ChassisSpeeds object representing the speeds in the robot frame
303   *     of reference. Positive x is towards the robot's front. Positive y is towards the robot's
304   *     left.
305   * @param robotAngle The angle of the robot as measured by a gyroscope. The robot's angle is
306   *     considered to be zero when it is facing directly away from your alliance station wall.
307   *     Remember that this should be CCW positive.
308   * @return ChassisSpeeds object representing the speeds in the field's frame of reference.
309   */
310  public static ChassisSpeeds fromRobotRelativeSpeeds(
311      ChassisSpeeds robotRelativeSpeeds, Rotation2d robotAngle) {
312    return fromRobotRelativeSpeeds(
313        robotRelativeSpeeds.vxMetersPerSecond,
314        robotRelativeSpeeds.vyMetersPerSecond,
315        robotRelativeSpeeds.omegaRadiansPerSecond,
316        robotAngle);
317  }
318
319  /**
320   * Adds two ChassisSpeeds and returns the sum.
321   *
322   * <p>For example, ChassisSpeeds{1.0, 0.5, 0.75} + ChassisSpeeds{2.0, 1.5, 0.25} =
323   * ChassisSpeeds{3.0, 2.0, 1.0}
324   *
325   * @param other The ChassisSpeeds to add.
326   * @return The sum of the ChassisSpeeds.
327   */
328  public ChassisSpeeds plus(ChassisSpeeds other) {
329    return new ChassisSpeeds(
330        vxMetersPerSecond + other.vxMetersPerSecond,
331        vyMetersPerSecond + other.vyMetersPerSecond,
332        omegaRadiansPerSecond + other.omegaRadiansPerSecond);
333  }
334
335  /**
336   * Subtracts the other ChassisSpeeds from the current ChassisSpeeds and returns the difference.
337   *
338   * <p>For example, ChassisSpeeds{5.0, 4.0, 2.0} - ChassisSpeeds{1.0, 2.0, 1.0} =
339   * ChassisSpeeds{4.0, 2.0, 1.0}
340   *
341   * @param other The ChassisSpeeds to subtract.
342   * @return The difference between the two ChassisSpeeds.
343   */
344  public ChassisSpeeds minus(ChassisSpeeds other) {
345    return new ChassisSpeeds(
346        vxMetersPerSecond - other.vxMetersPerSecond,
347        vyMetersPerSecond - other.vyMetersPerSecond,
348        omegaRadiansPerSecond - other.omegaRadiansPerSecond);
349  }
350
351  /**
352   * Returns the inverse of the current ChassisSpeeds. This is equivalent to negating all components
353   * of the ChassisSpeeds.
354   *
355   * @return The inverse of the current ChassisSpeeds.
356   */
357  public ChassisSpeeds unaryMinus() {
358    return new ChassisSpeeds(-vxMetersPerSecond, -vyMetersPerSecond, -omegaRadiansPerSecond);
359  }
360
361  /**
362   * Multiplies the ChassisSpeeds by a scalar and returns the new ChassisSpeeds.
363   *
364   * <p>For example, ChassisSpeeds{2.0, 2.5, 1.0} * 2 = ChassisSpeeds{4.0, 5.0, 1.0}
365   *
366   * @param scalar The scalar to multiply by.
367   * @return The scaled ChassisSpeeds.
368   */
369  public ChassisSpeeds times(double scalar) {
370    return new ChassisSpeeds(
371        vxMetersPerSecond * scalar, vyMetersPerSecond * scalar, omegaRadiansPerSecond * scalar);
372  }
373
374  /**
375   * Divides the ChassisSpeeds by a scalar and returns the new ChassisSpeeds.
376   *
377   * <p>For example, ChassisSpeeds{2.0, 2.5, 1.0} / 2 = ChassisSpeeds{1.0, 1.25, 0.5}
378   *
379   * @param scalar The scalar to divide by.
380   * @return The scaled ChassisSpeeds.
381   */
382  public ChassisSpeeds div(double scalar) {
383    return new ChassisSpeeds(
384        vxMetersPerSecond / scalar, vyMetersPerSecond / scalar, omegaRadiansPerSecond / scalar);
385  }
386
387  @Override
388  public final int hashCode() {
389    return Objects.hash(vxMetersPerSecond, vyMetersPerSecond, omegaRadiansPerSecond);
390  }
391
392  @Override
393  public boolean equals(Object o) {
394    return o == this
395        || o instanceof ChassisSpeeds c
396            && vxMetersPerSecond == c.vxMetersPerSecond
397            && vyMetersPerSecond == c.vyMetersPerSecond
398            && omegaRadiansPerSecond == c.omegaRadiansPerSecond;
399  }
400
401  @Override
402  public String toString() {
403    return String.format(
404        "ChassisSpeeds(Vx: %.2f m/s, Vy: %.2f m/s, Omega: %.2f rad/s)",
405        vxMetersPerSecond, vyMetersPerSecond, omegaRadiansPerSecond);
406  }
407}
